text:
Ukrainian tennis star Anhelina Kalinina has withdrawn from theParis Olympicsafter catching a cold during wetweatherin the French capital.

Kalinina was scheduled to come up against Arantxa Rus of Netherlands but pulled out due to illness on Sunday.

During Friday’s opening ceremony,athletes were paraded on boats along the River Seine in extremely wet conditions.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for latest news on the Paris Olympics

Showers continued on Saturday when Kalinina was scheduled to get her campaign underway but the rain forced tennis events on open courts to be rescheduled for Sunday.

‘Unfortunately, yesterday we spent a long time on the courts, waiting for the start of the competition,’ Kalinina said on Sunday in a statement from the Ukrainian committee.

‘It was raining all this time and this morning I felt a sore throat.

‘Of course, I was very upset, because it was a big dream and honour for me to play at the Olympics and represent my country, especially in such a difficult time. But we still decided not to take any risks to avoid complications.’

Kalinina was scheduled to make her Olympics debut.

Plans for tennis singles events were thrown into disarray by multiple withdrawals on Sunday.

World no6 Alex De Minaur was also forced to withdraw before play started on day two of action with Great Britain’s Cameron Norrie also ruled out.

Norrie was set to face Tallon Griekspoor but it was confirmed on Sunday morning he had been forced to withdraw due to injury.

Norrie’s withdrawal leaves Daniel Evans and Jack Draper as Team GB’s remaining medal hopes in the men’s singles competition.

Andy Murray is in doubles action alongside Evans on Sunday evening.

title: Olympics star pulls out of Paris event because she caught a cold | Metro News
description: Ukrainian tennis star Anhelina Kalinina has withdrawn from the Paris Olympics after catching a cold in the French capital.
